entries.name: --sf-z-base
entries.description: Base stacking level (1). Establishes a stacking context without elevation.

entries.name: --sf-z-below
entries.description: Z-index below stacking context base. For decorative pseudo-elements.

entries.name: --sf-z-dropdown
entries.description: Z-index for dropdown menus and select popups.

entries.name: --sf-z-fixed
entries.description: Z-index for position: fixed elements.

entries.name: --sf-z-modal
entries.description: Z-index for modal dialogs and their non-top-layer backdrop scrims. Sits above overlays and dropdowns. Default 1040.

entries.name: --sf-z-overlay
entries.description: Z-index for modal / dialog backdrops.

entries.name: --sf-z-raised
entries.description: Raised elevation (50). Cards, sticky headers.

entries.name: --sf-z-sticky
entries.description: Z-index for position: sticky elements (navbars, table headers).

entries.name: --sf-z-toast
entries.description: Z-index for toast / snackbar notifications.

entries.name: --sf-z-tooltip
entries.description: Z-index for tooltips and transient floating notifications. Highest named layer in the z-index stack. Default 1060.

entries.name: no-motion
entries.description: Suppresses all animations and transitions within the subtree. Apply to a container to create a reduced-motion zone without relying on the system preference.

entries.name: no-print
entries.description: Hides the element in @media print. Use on sidebars, navigation bars, and decorative elements that should not appear on paper.

entries.name: print-color-exact
entries.description: Forces exact color rendering in print (print-color-adjust: exact). Use on charts and colored badges that must preserve their fill colors in print.

entries.name: print-no-color
entries.description: Strips color information in print (print-color-adjust: economy). Defers to the printer's ink-saving mode for backgrounds and non-critical color fills.

entries.name: print-only
entries.description: Hides the element on screen (display: none) but shows it in @media print. Use for print-only headers, footers, and supplementary content not needed on screen.

entries.name: sf-alternate
entries.description: Zigzag / alternating two-column layout. Even children align image-left/text-right; odd children flip. Collapses to a single column below the breakpoint.

entries.name: sf-aspect
entries.description: Sets aspect-ratio from a --sf-aspect-ratio scoped token. Override the token inline to get any ratio without a new class.

entries.name: sf-bento
entries.description: Auto-fill bento grid for card dashboards. Children span 1 column by default; use span modifiers (sf-bento-wide, sf-bento-tall, sf-bento-full, sf-bento-featured) to break the grid.

entries.name: sf-bento--2
entries.description: Bento grid variant with a 2-column base layout.

entries.name: sf-bento--3
entries.description: Bento grid variant with a 3-column base layout.

entries.name: sf-bento--6
entries.description: Bento grid variant with a 6-column base layout.

entries.name: sf-bento--row-compact
entries.description: Bento grid variant with shorter default row height.

entries.name: sf-bento--row-tall
entries.description: Bento grid variant with taller default row height.

entries.name: sf-bento-featured
entries.description: Span modifier for a bento item: takes up 2×2 cells (featured hero placement).

entries.name: sf-bento-full
entries.description: Span modifier for a bento item: stretches across all columns (full-width banner).

entries.name: sf-bento-tall
entries.description: Span modifier for a bento item: doubles the row height (tall card).

entries.name: sf-bento-wide
entries.description: Span modifier for a bento item: spans 2 columns (wide card).

entries.name: sf-bg-layer
entries.description: Background layer — absolutely-positioned cover media that sits behind a parent's content. The parent is auto-promoted to a positioned, isolated stacking context via :has(), so .sf-bg-layer composes natively under .sf-scrim (background → scrim gradient → content). Works as a bare or as a wrapper with a nested > img / > video / > picture.

entries.name: sf-box
entries.description: Minimal padding container that applies --sf-component-pad on all sides. The simplest layout primitive for adding breathing room.

entries.name: sf-breakout
entries.description: Breaks a child element out of a parent sf-content-grid, spanning full bleed (or a named grid area) regardless of the parent column. Useful for full-width sections inside constrained prose.

entries.name: sf-btn
entries.description: Interactive call-to-action button. Apply to <button> or <a>. Intrinsic width by default; owns its own styling (excluded from the classless <button> look). Three orthogonal axes — colour family, style treatment (fill/--soft/--outline), gradient — plus a size modifier.

entries.name: sf-btn--action
entries.description: Button in the action colour family (same as the unmodified default, named explicitly).

entries.name: sf-btn--base
entries.description: Button in the base (surface-neutral) colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--block
entries.description: Full-width button (100% inline size) everywhere.

entries.name: sf-btn--block-cq
entries.description: Full-width button only inside a query container narrower than 20rem; intrinsic otherwise.

entries.name: sf-btn--danger
entries.description: Button in the danger (destructive) colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--gradient
entries.description: Gradient axis: paints the fill (or, with --outline, the border ring) with the family's --sf-gradient-* token. Core-4 brand families (primary/secondary/tertiary/action); solid no-op for other families. Composes with any colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--info
entries.description: Button in the info colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--l
entries.description: Large button size.

entries.name: sf-btn--neutral
entries.description: Button in the neutral colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--outline
entries.description: Outlined button (coloured border/text, transparent fill) that fills with the family colour on hover. Composes with any colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--primary
entries.description: Button in the primary brand colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--s
entries.description: Small button size.

entries.name: sf-btn--secondary
entries.description: Button in the secondary brand colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--soft
entries.description: Soft tonal-fill button: a light wash of the family colour (subtle at rest, muted on hover), coloured text, no border. Lower-emphasis than the solid fill. Composes with any colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--success
entries.description: Button in the success (positive) colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--tertiary
entries.description: Button in the tertiary brand colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--warning
entries.description: Button in the warning (caution) colour family.

entries.name: sf-btn--xl
entries.description: Extra-large button size.

entries.name: sf-btn--xs
entries.description: Extra-small button size.

entries.name: sf-card
entries.description: Padded content container with optional __header/__body/__footer and __media/__avatar/__title subcomponents. Uses concentric radius math so inner corners stay proportional.

entries.name: sf-card__avatar
entries.description: Circular avatar image inside a card, sized via --sf-card-avatar-size.

entries.name: sf-card__body
entries.description: Card body slot.

entries.name: sf-card__footer
entries.description: Card footer slot with a top divider.

entries.name: sf-card__header
entries.description: Card header slot with a bottom divider.

entries.name: sf-card__media
entries.description: Full-width cover image/video for a card, cropped to --sf-card-media-ratio (16/9 by default).

entries.name: sf-card__title
entries.description: Card title heading, sized via --sf-card-heading-size.

entries.name: sf-card--bordered
entries.description: Flat, outlined card: keeps the border, drops the shadow.

entries.name: sf-card--elevated
entries.description: Floating card: hides the border, applies a larger shadow.

entries.name: sf-card--interactive
entries.description: Adds a pointer cursor and a shadow/translate lift on hover or keyboard focus. Pair with .sf-clickable-parent for a fully clickable card. Lift is suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion.

entries.name: sf-center
entries.description: Centers children both horizontally and vertically using flexbox. Optionally add sf-center--intrinsic to size the container to its content.

entries.name: sf-center--intrinsic
entries.description: Modifier for sf-center: constrains the container to the natural width of its children (no stretching to fill available space).

entries.name: sf-clickable-parent
entries.description: Makes the entire card or list-item clickable via an absolutely-positioned child overlay link. Apply to the container; place sf-clickable-parent__overlay on the <a>.

entries.name: sf-clickable-parent__overlay
entries.description: The full-bleed overlay link inside an sf-clickable-parent. Stretches to cover the parent and uses pointer-events to let interactive children keep their own clicks.

entries.name: sf-cluster
entries.description: Flex-wrap row of variable-width items with automatic gap. Children wrap when the container is too narrow. Use for tag lists, button groups, and icon rows.

entries.name: sf-cluster--2xl
entries.description: Cluster variant with 2xl gap between items.

entries.name: sf-cluster--between
entries.description: Cluster variant that distributes items with space-between alignment.

entries.name: sf-cluster--center
entries.description: Cluster variant that centers the item row horizontally.

entries.name: sf-cluster--end
entries.description: Cluster variant that aligns items to the inline end (right in LTR).

entries.name: sf-cluster--l
entries.description: Cluster variant with large (l) gap between items.

entries.name: sf-cluster--m
entries.description: Cluster variant with medium (m) gap between items.

entries.name: sf-cluster--no-wrap
entries.description: Cluster variant that prevents item wrapping (single-line row).

entries.name: sf-cluster--s
entries.description: Cluster variant with small (s) gap between items.

entries.name: sf-cluster--xl
entries.description: Cluster variant with xl gap between items.

entries.name: sf-cluster--xs
entries.description: Cluster variant with xs gap between items.

entries.name: sf-color-pulse
entries.description: Applies a gentle color-pulse animation to draw attention to a dynamic value change (e.g. a counter updating). Scoped to users who have not requested reduced motion.

entries.name: sf-container
entries.description: Max-width page container, centered with auto side gutters. Establishes a named inline-size container (cq-container) for container queries. Default width is --sf-container-m.

entries.name: sf-container--full
entries.description: Container variant that spans the full viewport width (no max-width cap).

entries.name: sf-container--narrow
entries.description: Container variant with a narrower max-width (--sf-container-s). Use for single-column prose and forms.

entries.name: sf-container--prose
entries.description: Container variant with a prose-optimised max-width (--sf-container-prose). Ideal for long-form reading content.

entries.name: sf-container--wide
entries.description: Container variant with a wider max-width (--sf-container-l). Use for dashboards and wide media.

entries.name: sf-content-auto
entries.description: Sets content-visibility: auto on the element. The browser skips rendering off-screen content, improving LCP for long pages.

entries.name: sf-content-grid
entries.description: Holy-grail content grid with named areas: full-bleed, popout, and content columns. Use sf-breakout on children to escape the content column.

entries.name: sf-cover
entries.description: Vertically stretches to at least a viewport height with a centered element (sf-cover__center). Typical use: hero sections.

entries.name: sf-cover__center
entries.description: The centered focal element inside an sf-cover. Stays vertically centered even when the cover content is shorter than the viewport.

entries.name: sf-cover--max
entries.description: Cover variant that uses a max-height instead of min-height for the viewport constraint.

entries.name: sf-cover--min
entries.description: Cover variant that reduces the minimum height to half a viewport.

entries.name: sf-cover--padding-l
entries.description: Cover variant with large vertical padding above and below the centered content.

entries.name: sf-cover--padding-s
entries.description: Cover variant with small vertical padding above and below the centered content.

entries.name: sf-cq
entries.description: Assigns an inline-size container context to any element. Children can then use @container rules or container-query-aware tokens.

entries.name: sf-divide
entries.description: Rules between direct children (a border on each child after the first), reusing .sf-divider's tokens — no per-gap markup needed.

entries.name: sf-divide--vertical
entries.description: Vertical rules between direct children (inline-start borders), for row/horizontal layouts.

entries.name: sf-divider
entries.description: Horizontal rule (divider line). Uses --sf-divider-* tokens for color, width, and style. Modifiers change dash style or orientation.

entries.name: sf-divider--dashed
entries.description: Divider variant with a dashed stroke.

entries.name: sf-divider--dotted
entries.description: Divider variant with a dotted stroke.

entries.name: sf-divider--gradient
entries.description: Divider variant rendered as a gradient that fades to transparent at both ends.

entries.name: sf-divider--soft
entries.description: Divider variant with reduced opacity (subtle separator).

entries.name: sf-divider--strong
entries.description: Divider variant with stronger/darker color for a more prominent separator.

entries.name: sf-divider--vertical
entries.description: Divider variant rendered as a vertical line. Use inside flex or grid containers.

entries.name: sf-drop-shadow-l
entries.description: Large filter drop-shadow — follows the element's alpha shape (--sf-drop-shadow-l).

entries.name: sf-drop-shadow-m
entries.description: Medium filter drop-shadow — follows the element's alpha shape (--sf-drop-shadow-m).

entries.name: sf-drop-shadow-s
entries.description: Small filter drop-shadow — follows the element's alpha shape (--sf-drop-shadow-s).

entries.name: sf-drop-shadow-xl
entries.description: Extra-large filter drop-shadow — follows the element's alpha shape (--sf-drop-shadow-xl).

entries.name: sf-drop-shadow-xs
entries.description: Extra-small filter drop-shadow — follows the element's alpha shape (--sf-drop-shadow-xs).

entries.name: sf-entrance--fade
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in as it enters the viewport. Animation tied to scroll progress via animation-timeline.

entries.name: sf-entrance--fade-down
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving downward into position.

entries.name: sf-entrance--fade-left
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving from the right toward the left.

entries.name: sf-entrance--fade-right
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving from the left toward the right.

entries.name: sf-entrance--fade-up
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving upward into position.

entries.name: sf-entrance--scale-up
entries.description: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while scaling up from a slightly smaller size.

entries.name: sf-equal
entries.description: Flowing CSS multi-column layout (not a grid) — content distributes across columns like a newspaper instead of sitting in fixed cells. Reach for .sf-grid/.sf-grid--fit for discrete grid cells instead.

entries.name: sf-equal--2
entries.description: Equal-columns variant locked to column-count: 2.

entries.name: sf-equal--3
entries.description: Equal-columns variant locked to column-count: 3.

entries.name: sf-equal--4
entries.description: Equal-columns variant locked to column-count: 4.

entries.name: sf-equal--6
entries.description: Equal-columns variant locked to column-count: 6.

entries.name: sf-equal-height
entries.description: Forces all flex children to equal height (align-items: stretch). Use on a flex row to make cards in a grid share the tallest card's height.

entries.name: sf-exit--fade
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades the element out in place (sf-fade-out).

entries.name: sf-exit--fade-down
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades out while sliding downward (sf-slide-out-down).

entries.name: sf-exit--fade-left
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades out while sliding to the left (sf-slide-out-left).

entries.name: sf-exit--fade-right
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades out while sliding to the right (sf-slide-out-right).

entries.name: sf-exit--fade-up
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades out while sliding upward (sf-slide-out-up).

entries.name: sf-exit--scale-down
entries.description: Scroll-out exit animation: fades out while shrinking (sf-scale-down).

entries.name: sf-fade-in
entries.description: One-shot fade-in animation (opacity 0 → 1). Scoped to no-preference so it stops automatically for reduced-motion users.

entries.name: sf-fade-out
entries.description: One-shot fade-out animation (opacity 1 → 0). Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-flow
entries.description: Applies --sf-flow-space as margin-block-start to all direct children except the first. Establishes consistent vertical rhythm in prose-like containers.

entries.name: sf-fluid-cq
entries.description: .sf-fluid-cq: opt a subtree into a CONTAINER-relative fluid scale (issue #497). By default --sf-text-* / --sf-space-* interpolate against 100vw (the viewport); inside .sf-fluid-cq they interpolate against 100cqi (this box's inline size), so type + spacing track the container…

entries.name: sf-focus-parent
entries.description: Forwards focus-visible styling to this container when any descendant is keyboard-focused. Useful for custom controls that wrap a visually-hidden <input>.

entries.name: sf-focus-shadow
entries.description: Applies the standard focus ring as a box-shadow to any element. Use on custom controls where the native :focus-visible ring doesn't render correctly.

entries.name: sf-frame
entries.description: Aspect-ratio container for media (images, video, maps). Children fill and cover the fixed-ratio box. Default ratio is 16:9.

entries.name: sf-frame--3-2
entries.description: Frame variant locked to a 3:2 aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-frame--4-3
entries.description: Frame variant locked to a 4:3 aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-frame--cinema
entries.description: Frame variant locked to a cinematic 21:9 aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-frame--golden
entries.description: Frame variant locked to the golden ratio (1.618:1).

entries.name: sf-frame--portrait
entries.description: Frame variant locked to a portrait 2:3 aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-frame--square
entries.description: Frame variant locked to a square 1:1 aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-frame--video
entries.description: Frame variant locked to the standard 16:9 video aspect ratio.

entries.name: sf-full-bleed
entries.description: Forces an element to span the full viewport width, breaking out of any centered container. Use for full-bleed images and section backgrounds.

entries.name: sf-gap
entries.description: Injects the default --sf-gap into an existing flex or grid container without imposing display:flex itself. Use when the parent is already a flex or grid.

entries.name: sf-gap--2xl
entries.description: Gap injection variant with 2xl gap value.

entries.name: sf-gap--l
entries.description: Gap injection variant with large (l) gap value.

entries.name: sf-gap--m
entries.description: Gap injection variant with medium (m) gap value.

entries.name: sf-gap--s
entries.description: Gap injection variant with small (s) gap value.

entries.name: sf-gap--xl
entries.description: Gap injection variant with xl gap value.

entries.name: sf-gap--xs
entries.description: Gap injection variant with xs gap value.

entries.name: sf-grid
entries.description: Auto-fill CSS grid. Columns are sized with RAM pattern (Repeat, Auto-fill, Minmax) and wrap automatically. Default column min-width is --sf-grid-min-col.

entries.name: sf-grid--2xl
entries.description: Grid variant with a 2xl minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid--dense
entries.description: Grid variant with grid-auto-flow: dense, allowing the grid to fill holes created by span modifiers.

entries.name: sf-grid--fit
entries.description: Grid variant that switches from auto-fill to auto-fit, collapsing empty columns.

entries.name: sf-grid--l
entries.description: Grid variant with a large (l) minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid--m
entries.description: Grid variant with a medium (m) minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid--s
entries.description: Grid variant with a small (s) minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid--xl
entries.description: Grid variant with an xl minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid--xs
entries.description: Grid variant with an xs minimum column width.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-1
entries.description: Single-column layout (forces all children to one column).

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-1-2
entries.description: Two-column ratio grid: first child takes 1fr, second takes 2fr. Container-query responsive.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-1-3
entries.description: Two-column ratio grid: first child takes 1fr, second takes 3fr. Container-query responsive.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-2
entries.description: Responsive 2-column fixed grid. Uses container queries to collapse to one column on narrow containers.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-2-1
entries.description: Two-column ratio grid: first child takes 2fr, second takes 1fr. Container-query responsive.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-3
entries.description: Responsive 3-column fixed grid. Collapses progressively on narrow containers.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-3-1
entries.description: Two-column ratio grid: first child takes 3fr, second takes 1fr. Container-query responsive.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-4
entries.description: Responsive 4-column fixed grid. Collapses progressively on narrow containers.

entries.name: sf-grid-cols-6
entries.description: Responsive 6-column fixed grid. Collapses progressively on narrow containers.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex
entries.description: Flexbox alternative to .sf-grid for item counts that don't divide evenly: leftover last-row items stretch to fill the row by default, or stay fixed-width and centered with --center.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--2xl
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with a 2xl minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--center
entries.description: Grid-flex variant that keeps last-row leftover items fixed-width and centered instead of stretching.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--l
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with a large (l) minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--m
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with a medium (m) minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--s
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with a small (s) minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--xl
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with an xl minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-grid-flex--xs
entries.description: Grid-flex variant with an xs minimum item width.

entries.name: sf-h1
entries.description: Applies h1-level typography (size, line-height, weight, tracking, max-width) to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-h2
entries.description: Applies h2-level typography to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-h3
entries.description: Applies h3-level typography to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-h4
entries.description: Applies h4-level typography to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-h5
entries.description: Applies h5-level typography to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-h6
entries.description: Applies h6-level typography to any element without changing its semantic level.

entries.name: sf-header--l
entries.description: Header block padding at the l step (--sf-space-l).

entries.name: sf-header--m
entries.description: Header block padding at the m step (--sf-space-m).

entries.name: sf-header--s
entries.description: Header block padding at the s step (--sf-space-s).

entries.name: sf-header--xl
entries.description: Header block padding at the xl step (--sf-space-xl).

entries.name: sf-header--xs
entries.description: Header block padding at the xs step (--sf-space-xs).

entries.name: sf-hover-float
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: translates the element up slightly on hover.

entries.name: sf-hover-grow
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: scales the element up slightly on hover.

entries.name: sf-hover-shrink
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: scales the element down slightly on hover.

entries.name: sf-hover-sink
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: translates the element down slightly on hover.

entries.name: sf-hover-slide-end
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: translates the element toward the inline-end on hover.

entries.name: sf-hover-slide-start
entries.description: Hover-triggered transform: translates the element toward the inline-start on hover.

entries.name: sf-icon
entries.description: Inline icon sizing primitive. Sets width and height to --sf-icon-m via em-based tokens so the icon scales with the surrounding text.

entries.name: sf-icon--2xl
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-2xl (extra-extra-large).

entries.name: sf-icon--boxed
entries.description: Icon variant with a square bordered box background. Useful for standalone icon buttons and feature icons.

entries.name: sf-icon--l
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-l (large).

entries.name: sf-icon--m
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-m (medium, same as base sf-icon).

entries.name: sf-icon--s
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-s (small).

entries.name: sf-icon--xl
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-xl (extra-large).

entries.name: sf-icon--xs
entries.description: Icon variant sized to --sf-icon-xs (extra-small).

entries.name: sf-imposter
entries.description: Absolutely positions an element at the center of its nearest positioned ancestor. Use for overlays, badges, and floating labels.

entries.name: sf-imposter--contain
entries.description: Imposter variant that clamps the element within the bounds of its parent using overflow:hidden on the parent.

entries.name: sf-imposter--fixed
entries.description: Imposter variant that uses position:fixed instead of absolute. Use for viewport-level overlays and modals.

entries.name: sf-is-active
entries.description: Active state — applied to the currently interacted-with element (e.g. pressed button, active menu item). Sets --sf-is-active to 1 for calc() branching.

entries.name: sf-is-busy
entries.description: Busy state — shows a progress cursor to indicate background processing. Does not mask content; use sf-is-loading when content should be hidden during loading.

entries.name: sf-is-collapsed
entries.description: Collapsed state for disclosure widgets — hides the expandable region. Pair with sf-is-expanded or sf-is-open. Components add transitions.

entries.name: sf-is-current
entries.description: Current/selected state — marks the active page in navigation or the selected item in a list. Sets --sf-is-current to 1 for calc() branching.

entries.name: sf-is-danger
entries.description: Danger status state — applies danger color tokens for destructive actions (e.g. a delete confirmation dialog) and form validation errors.

entries.name: sf-is-disabled
entries.description: Disabled state — dims the element (--sf-opacity-disabled), removes pointer events, and sets cursor: not-allowed. Inherited by child elements that re-enable pointer events.

entries.name: sf-is-draggable
entries.description: Draggable state — shows a grab cursor to indicate the element can be dragged. Apply before a drag interaction begins.

entries.name: sf-is-dragging
entries.description: Active drag state — shows a grabbing cursor while the element is being dragged. Apply during the drag event.

entries.name: sf-is-drop-target
entries.description: Drop-target state — applies a visual highlight to indicate a valid drag-and-drop destination. Toggle on drag-over events.

entries.name: sf-is-empty
entries.description: Empty state — hides the element when it has no child nodes (:empty). Used for dynamic lists or containers that may render empty.

entries.name: sf-is-error
entries.description: Error state — alias for sf-is-danger. Applies danger color tokens to indicate a validation failure or system error. Visual styling is identical to sf-is-invalid.

entries.name: sf-is-expanded
entries.description: Expanded state for disclosure widgets — shows the expandable region. Inverse of sf-is-collapsed. Components add transitions.

entries.name: sf-is-hidden
entries.description: Hidden state — removes the element from layout and accessibility tree (display: none !important).

entries.name: sf-is-highlighted
entries.description: Highlighted state — applies a highlight background. Useful for search result highlighting and keyboard-navigated lists.

entries.name: sf-is-info
entries.description: Info status state — applies info color tokens. Use for neutral informational messages and hints.

entries.name: sf-is-invalid
entries.description: Invalid state — applies danger color tokens to form fields with validation failures. Identical visual output to sf-is-error; the name signals form-field context.

entries.name: sf-is-invisible
entries.description: Invisible state — hides the element visually but keeps it in the layout and accessibility tree (visibility: hidden).

entries.name: sf-is-loading
entries.description: Loading state — hides the element's text (color: transparent), removes pointer events, and renders a spinner via ::after. Use for buttons and containers awaiting async results.

entries.name: sf-is-open
entries.description: Open/shown state for modals, dropdowns, and drawers — counterpart to sf-is-collapsed/sf-is-hidden. Sets --sf-is-open to 1 for calc() branching.

entries.name: sf-is-pending
entries.description: Pending state for optimistic UI — content remains visible but dimmed while an async request is in flight. Differs from sf-is-loading (which hides content entirely).

entries.name: sf-is-pressed
entries.description: Pressed/toggled-on state for toggle buttons. Sets --sf-is-pressed to 1 for calc() branching and signals the selected state to components.

entries.name: sf-is-readonly
entries.description: Readonly state — removes pointer events and text selection. Use for fields that display data but cannot be edited.

entries.name: sf-is-selected
entries.description: Selected state — marks an item as selected in a list, table row, or grid cell. Visual specifics (background, border) are applied by component styles.

entries.name: sf-is-skeleton
entries.description: Skeleton loading placeholder state. Applies a shimmer animation over the element to indicate that content is loading. Works on both img and non-media elements.

entries.name: sf-is-success
entries.description: Success status state — applies success color tokens. Use for confirmation messages, completed form steps, and positive feedback.

entries.name: sf-is-valid
entries.description: Valid state — applies success color tokens to form fields that have passed validation. Visual styling is identical to sf-is-success; the name signals form-field context.

entries.name: sf-is-visible
entries.description: Visible state — forces the element to be visible (visibility: visible). Use to un-hide an element that inherited visibility: hidden from a parent.

entries.name: sf-is-warning
entries.description: Warning status state — applies warning color tokens. Use for cautionary messages and non-critical alerts.

entries.name: sf-line-clamp-2
entries.description: Clamps text to 2 lines with an ellipsis. Applies -webkit-line-clamp: 2.

entries.name: sf-line-clamp-3
entries.description: Clamps text to 3 lines with an ellipsis. Applies -webkit-line-clamp: 3.

entries.name: sf-line-clamp-N
entries.description: Clamps text to a custom N lines. Set --sf-line-clamp on the element to control the number of visible lines.

entries.name: sf-link--reverse
entries.description: Link color modifier: reverses the default link color to work on dark/inverted backgrounds. Sets color to --sf-color-text--inverse.

entries.name: sf-link--subtle
entries.description: Link color modifier: renders the link in the body text color instead of the default action color. Underline still indicates the link on hover.

entries.name: sf-link-external
entries.description: Automatically appends an external-link icon (via ::after content) to indicate the link opens in a new tab or goes to an external domain.

entries.name: sf-list-none
entries.description: Drops marker + inline start-padding in one class, on any <ul>/<ol> (or element with list-style applied).

entries.name: sf-live-validate
entries.description: Scopes native constraint-validation feedback to this subtree: within it, :user-invalid/:user-valid on input/select/textarea drive --sf-field-border-color (the same token .sf-is-invalid/.sf-is-valid set explicitly). Apply to a <form> or <fieldset>. Off by default so a still-empty required field isn't marked invalid before a submit is attempted.

entries.name: sf-marker--action
entries.description: Colours list ::markers with the action colour.

entries.name: sf-marker--primary
entries.description: Colours list ::markers with the primary brand colour.

entries.name: sf-marker--secondary
entries.description: Colours list ::markers with the secondary brand colour.

entries.name: sf-marker--tertiary
entries.description: Colours list ::markers with the tertiary brand colour.

entries.name: sf-no-tap-highlight
entries.description: Removes the mobile tap highlight color (-webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent). Use on interactive elements with a custom active state.

entries.name: sf-not-prose
entries.description: Opt-out of sf-prose typography styles within a prose block. Apply to an element that should keep default browser or component styles.

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade
entries.description: Adds a gradient fade at the inline-end of an overflowing element to hint at hidden content. Use inside sf-reel or any scroll container.

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--block
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades both the top and bottom edges simultaneously (block axis).

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--bottom
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades the bottom (block-end) edge.

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--inline
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades both the left and right edges simultaneously (inline axis).

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--left
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades the left (inline-start) edge.

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--right
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades the right (inline-end) edge. Explicit alias for the default sf-overflow-fade behaviour.

entries.name: sf-overflow-fade--top
entries.description: Overflow fade variant: fades the top (block-start) edge.

entries.name: sf-overlap
entries.description: Overlap recipe — pulls the element upward (negative block-start margin) so it overlaps the previous sibling by --sf-overlap-pull.

entries.name: sf-overlap--down
entries.description: Overlap recipe — pulls following content up (negative block-end margin) so this element overlaps the sibling below it.

entries.name: sf-overlap--end
entries.description: Overlap recipe — pulls the element toward the inline-end (negative inline-end margin) to overlap horizontally.

entries.name: sf-overlap--start
entries.description: Overlap recipe — pulls the element toward the inline-start (negative inline-start margin) to overlap horizontally.

entries.name: sf-overlap-host
entries.description: Card-container recipe — the receiving side of an overlap: an isolated stacking context with block-start padding compensation so in-flow content clears an element intruding from above. The padding knob defaults to the pull knob so one override tunes both.

entries.name: sf-overlay
entries.description: Overlay layout helper — absolutely fills its positioned parent (`position: absolute; inset: 0`) for overlays, backdrops, and media scrims.

entries.name: sf-pancake
entries.description: Three-row layout (header, main, footer) where main takes all remaining vertical space via flex-grow. The footer always sticks to the bottom on tall viewports.

entries.name: sf-prose
entries.description: Opinionated typographic defaults for long-form content: heading hierarchy, paragraph spacing, blockquote, code, and list styling. Override with sf-not-prose.

entries.name: sf-reel
entries.description: Horizontally scrollable row of fixed-height items with snap scrolling. Children don't shrink below their intrinsic width, creating a card carousel effect.

entries.name: sf-scale-down
entries.description: One-shot scale-down animation (element shrinks from full size). Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-scale-up
entries.description: One-shot scale-up animation (element grows from smaller size). Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-scrim
entries.description: Overlay container with a gradient darkening scrim behind text placed on an image or colored background.

entries.name: sf-scrim__content
entries.description: Content area inside an sf-scrim. Positioned above the gradient overlay layer.

entries.name: sf-scrim--bottom
entries.description: Scrim variant with the gradient emanating from the bottom edge (bottom-to-top fade).

entries.name: sf-scrim--full
entries.description: Scrim variant that covers the entire surface with a uniform translucent overlay.

entries.name: sf-scrim--top
entries.description: Scrim variant with the gradient emanating from the top edge (top-to-bottom fade).

entries.name: sf-scroll-shadow
entries.description: Adds inset box-shadows at the top and bottom of a scrollable container to indicate hidden overflow above or below.

entries.name: sf-scroll-snap
entries.description: Enables CSS scroll-snap-type: x mandatory on a horizontal scroll container. Children should have scroll-snap-align.

entries.name: sf-section
entries.description: Full-width page section with vertical padding (--sf-section-pad-default). Use to create visually distinct content bands.

entries.name: sf-section--2xl
entries.description: Section variant with 2xl vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section--collapse
entries.description: Section variant that removes vertical padding (zero-padding section for adjacent same-color blocks).

entries.name: sf-section--guttered
entries.description: Adds horizontal page gutters directly to a section — use when you want to skip a separate .sf-container wrapper (gutterless layout inside section). Neutralises container gutter to prevent double padding.

entries.name: sf-section--l
entries.description: Section variant with large (l) vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section--m
entries.description: Section variant with medium (m) vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section--s
entries.description: Section variant with small (s) vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section--xl
entries.description: Section variant with xl vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section--xs
entries.description: Section variant with xs vertical padding.

entries.name: sf-section-group
entries.description: Groups adjacent sf-section elements so their combined padding collapses correctly at the top and bottom edges.

entries.name: sf-selection--alt
entries.description: Overrides the ::selection custom properties (core/base.css) on the element and its descendants, for surfaces where the default selection colours land with poor contrast (e.g. a dark card on a light page). Backed by --sf-color-selection-*--alt (core/tokens.css).

entries.name: sf-sidebar
entries.description: Two-column sidebar layout: first child is the sidebar, second child is main content. The sidebar collapses below the breakpoint (--sf-sidebar-min-width). Default sidebar on the left.

entries.name: sf-sidebar--narrow
entries.description: Sidebar variant with a narrower sidebar column.

entries.name: sf-sidebar--right
entries.description: Sidebar variant with the sidebar on the right (second child becomes the sidebar).

entries.name: sf-sidebar--wide
entries.description: Sidebar variant with a wider sidebar column.

entries.name: sf-slide-in-down
entries.description: One-shot slide-in-from-above animation. Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-slide-in-left
entries.description: One-shot slide-in-from-right animation (slides to the left). Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-slide-in-right
entries.description: One-shot slide-in-from-left animation (slides to the right). Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-slide-in-up
entries.description: One-shot slide-in-from-below animation. Scoped to no-preference.

entries.name: sf-stack
entries.description: Flex column with even vertical spacing between children (--sf-stack-gap). Stacking context for vertical rhythm.

entries.name: sf-stack--2xl
entries.description: Stack variant with 2xl gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stack--center
entries.description: Stack variant that centers children on the cross axis.

entries.name: sf-stack--end
entries.description: Stack variant that aligns children to the cross-axis end (right in LTR).

entries.name: sf-stack--l
entries.description: Stack variant with large (l) gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stack--m
entries.description: Stack variant with medium (m) gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stack--s
entries.description: Stack variant with small (s) gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stack--stretch
entries.description: Stack variant that stretches children to fill the full available width.

entries.name: sf-stack--xl
entries.description: Stack variant with xl gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stack--xs
entries.description: Stack variant with xs gap between children.

entries.name: sf-stagger
entries.description: Stagger — choreography, not the animation itself. Put .sf-stagger on a PARENT; every direct child gets an incrementing animation-delay so a time-based entrance (.sf-fade-in / .sf-slide-in-*) plays in sequence. Children WITHOUT an animation just carry an inert delay (no-op), so…

entries.name: sf-sticky
entries.description: position: sticky pinned at the header-aware --sf-sticky-offset.

entries.name: sf-sticky--l
entries.description: Sticky positioning with an extra large gap (--sf-space-l) above the header-aware offset.

entries.name: sf-sticky--m
entries.description: Sticky positioning with an extra medium gap (--sf-space-m) above the header-aware offset.

entries.name: sf-sticky--s
entries.description: Sticky positioning with an extra small gap (--sf-space-s) above the header-aware offset.

entries.name: sf-subgrid
entries.description: Passes the parent grid columns through to children via subgrid. Place on a grid item that should align its own children to the outer grid columns.

entries.name: sf-subgrid-rows
entries.description: Passes the parent grid rows through to children via subgrid. Place on a grid item that should align its own children to the outer grid rows.

entries.name: sf-surface
entries.description: Generic semantic surface: applies --sf-surface-color as the background and automatically sets a contrasting text color. Tone variants (--primary, --action, etc.) activate preset palettes.

entries.name: sf-surface--action
entries.description: Surface variant using the action color palette (button/CTA primary color).

entries.name: sf-surface--danger
entries.description: Surface variant using the danger status palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--info
entries.description: Surface variant using the info status palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--inverse
entries.description: Surface variant that inverts light/dark, creating an always-dark surface in light mode and always-light in dark mode.

entries.name: sf-surface--neutral
entries.description: Surface variant using the neutral palette (muted/gray).

entries.name: sf-surface--primary
entries.description: Surface variant using the primary brand color palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--secondary
entries.description: Surface variant using the secondary brand color palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--success
entries.description: Surface variant using the success status palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--tertiary
entries.description: Surface variant using the tertiary brand color palette.

entries.name: sf-surface--warning
entries.description: Surface variant using the warning status palette.

entries.name: sf-surface-bg
entries.description: Named background surface preset (issue #523). Composes the --sf-surface-bg-* token set (see tokens.macros.css) into one reusable, named background: base colour + image/gradient/pattern, sizing, an optional overlay layered above the image, and an optional animation. Inert until…

entries.name: sf-switcher
entries.description: Flex layout that switches from a horizontal row to a vertical column when the container is narrower than --sf-switcher-threshold.

entries.name: sf-switcher--no-wrap
entries.description: Switcher variant that stays horizontal and never wraps (single-line regardless of container width).

entries.name: sf-switcher--vertical
entries.description: Switcher variant that starts in the vertical (stacked) direction on all sizes.

entries.name: sf-tabular-nums
entries.description: Forces tabular (monospaced) number rendering. Use on price tables, counters, and anywhere numbers need to align vertically across rows.

entries.name: sf-text-2xl
entries.description: Applies the 2xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-2xs
entries.description: Applies the 2xs text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-3xl
entries.description: Applies the 3xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-4xl
entries.description: Applies the 4xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-gradient
entries.description: Applies a brand gradient to inline text. Sets the background to the gradient, clips it to the text, and makes the text color transparent.

entries.name: sf-text-l
entries.description: Applies the l text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-m
entries.description: Applies the m (body) text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-protect
entries.description: Wraps text in a translucent dark gradient overlay to ensure legibility when placed over a light or variable background image.

entries.name: sf-text-s
entries.description: Applies the s text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-xl
entries.description: Applies the xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-text-xs
entries.description: Applies the xs text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.

entries.name: sf-theme-dark
entries.description: Forces dark colour scheme on the element and its subtree (class alias of [data-theme="dark"]).

entries.name: sf-theme-light
entries.description: Forces light colour scheme on the element and its subtree (class alias of [data-theme="light"]).

entries.name: sf-theme-transition
entries.description: Opt-in animated light/dark theme crossfade. Apply to <html> (or a subtree) so color tokens transition smoothly when [data-theme] changes. Duration controlled by --sf-theme-transition-duration.

entries.name: sf-truncate
entries.description: Single-line text overflow with ellipsis. Equivalent to the classic text-overflow: ellipsis trio (white-space nowrap, overflow hidden, text-overflow ellipsis).

entries.name: skip-link
entries.description: Accessible skip-navigation link. Hidden until focused; jumps keyboard users past repeated navigation blocks. Place as the very first element in <body>.

entries.name: sr-only
entries.description: Visually hides content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Use for labels, descriptions, and off-screen text that assistive technology needs.

entries.name: sr-only-focusable
entries.description: Extends sr-only — also reveals the element when it receives keyboard focus. Typical use: skip links and visually-hidden focus targets.
